Stone Cottage Care Center

CCN: 165381 · Sigourney, IA 52591 · Keokuk County

Overview

Address
900 South Stone Street, Sigourney, IA 52591
Phone
6416222971
Certified beds
41
Avg daily residents
29 (70% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1998-01-01
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.17 3.14 3.89 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.46 0.55 0.56 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.76 0.70 0.93 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.95 1.89 2.40 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.21 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.77
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.42
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.40
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
60.7%
annual
RN turnover
80.0%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.11
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.81
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-04 15 11 8 104 1 104
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-09-26 37 11 28 315 1 315
Total weighted health score
156.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (64)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
60 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-12-23 2026-01-03
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-12-23 2026-01-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-11-05 2025-11-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-09-04 2025-09-26
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-03-24 2025-04-02
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-12-31 2025-01-13
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-12-31 2025-01-13
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. C 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0606 Not hire anyone with a finding of abuse, neglect, exploitation, or theft. D 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-09-26 2024-10-18
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2024-05-22 2024-06-15
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2024-01-25 2024-02-16
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-01-25 2024-02-16
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-01-25 2024-02-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-01-25 2024-02-16
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2024-01-25 2024-02-20
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-01-25 2024-02-20
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0926 Have policies on smoking. D 2024-01-10 2024-02-20
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-11-30 2023-12-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. J 2023-11-30 2024-02-20
